title: Kaaldar

{{Quote|Luke has Bledsoe's disease! You're a doctor! You can't just take an ocular print... and let him die!|Anduvil, to Kaaldar|<ref name="sojourn" />}}
'''Kaaldar''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Doctor/Legends|doctor]] in the employ of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Kaaldar genetically modified the highly infectious illness known as [[Bledsoe's disease]] as part of a intelligence program targeting the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], which caused [[Planet/Legends|world]]-devastating outbreaks. Rebel agents [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Anduvil]] eventually discovered Kaaldar's plot on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] world of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], and destroyed his laboratory.

==Biography==
{{Quote|A discoloration of the victim's eyes is a symptom of Bledsoe's disease! By genetic manipulation of the virus, we can encode a message in that symptom! The virus becomes an undetectable courier of star charts... revealing Rebel bases for the Imperial space fleet to destroy!|Kaaldar|<ref name="sojourn" />}}
An expert in infectious diseases, [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] Kaaldar was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who used his medical knowledge in service of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], Kaaldar became one of the central figures in an Imperial plot that stretched across several different [[Planet/Legends|worlds]].<ref name="sojourn" /> At the behest of the Empire, Kaaldar engineered a strain of the highly contagious [[Bledsoe's disease]]<ref name="cswe">{{CSWECite|II|189|Kaaldar, Dr.}}</ref> that Imperial leadership hoped could help them win the war against the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]]. Bledsoe's disease was a deadly illness with one unique symptom: afflicted patients developed a distinctive discoloration of the [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. Kaaldar genetically manipulated the virus to encode a message in that symptom, creating something he called the "window effect": the victim's eyes, instead of their typical discoloration, turned into [[Star map/Legends|star maps]], that the Empire would in turn examine to find secret Rebel bases for the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] to target and destroy.<ref name="sojourn" />

[[File:BledsoeSerum.jpg|thumb|left|220px|Kaaldar administers a serum for Bledsoe's disease to Luke Skywalker.]]
Using [[Squill (creature)|squills]], a non-[[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] creature that could carry Bledsoe's disease, the Empire purposely started outbreaks on several worlds thought to be leaning towards supporting the rebellion, both to undermine the planet's social order and to discover locations of Rebel bases. After an outbreak of Kaaldar's modified disease decimated the populace of [[Tay'ah'loo]], Kaaldar moved his operation to a base on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] world of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. It wasn't long before the Bledsoe's disease plot was noticed by Rebel [[Alliance Intelligence/Legends|intelligence]], who sent [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], the hero of the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] and a native Tatooinian, back to his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] to investigate alongside an [[Ogemite/Legends|Ogemite]] operative named [[Anduvil]].<ref name="sojourn" />

After tracking the outbreak into the [[Dune Sea/Legends|Dune Sea]], Skywalker was inadvertently infected with Bledsoe's disease, leading to his capture by [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]]. The Imperial soldiers took the two Rebels back to their base, where Kaaldar refused to treat Skywalker and instead began examining the stars in his eyes. However, Skywalker and Anduvil overpowered their stormtrooper guards and took Kaaldar hostage, forcing him to administer a cure and reveal the secrets of the Bledsoe's plot. Skywalker and Anduvil escaped afterward, and soon put an end to Kaaldar's scheme by blowing up the entire Imperial base.<ref name="sojourn" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|His eyes are windows, Ogem woman... windows into the Empire's secrets! While the condition persists, he cannot be allowed to live!|Kaaldar, regarding Luke Skywalker|<ref name="sojourn" />}}
Although Kaaldar was a medical doctor, he held no concern for the welfare of the strain of Bledsoe's disease he engineered: he cared more for the mission of the Galactic Empire, and the information that could be gleaned from their symptoms. Kaaldar planned to completely withhold treatment from Luke Skywalker to that end, but quickly acquiesced when he and Anduvil threatened him with violence.<ref name="sojourn" />

==Behind the scenes==
Kaaldar first appeared in ''[[Tatooine Sojourn]]'', a [[Star Wars (comic strip)|comic strip]] story arc written by [[Steve Gerber]] and released in [[1979]]. Kaaldar was illustrated by [[Russ Manning]].<ref name="sojourn" />
